instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Fetch All Apex SOAP Metadata</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, DataSense is performed for all Apex classes in the organization. If <code>false</code>, DataSense is performed only for classes in <strong>Apex Class Names</strong>. If the organization contains many Apex classes, this might cause a <code>ConnectionTimeout</code> during DataSense.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Fetch All Apex REST Metadata</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, DataSense is performed for all Apex classes in the organization. If <code>false</code>, DataSense is performed only for classes in <strong>Apex Class Names</strong>. If the organization contains many Apex classes, this might cause a <code>ConnectionTimeout</code> during DataSense.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Apex Class Names</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of Apex class names involved in metadata retrieval via DataSense.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Include null values</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Includes null values in the Apex Rest API request. This parameter applies to the <strong>Invoke Apex Rest Method</strong> operation only.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Basic Authentication Connection Type (Supported by Salesforce API v64.0 and below)

Uses a username and a password for authentication.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when establishing a connection to the remote service. Values less than one millisecond are converted to <code>0</code>. A value of <code>0</code> means wait indefinitely.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>0</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> parameter.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Login Request Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the consumer waits to receive the login request before the request times out. Values less than one millisecond are converted to <code>0</code>. A default value of <code>0</code> means an indefinite wait, so set a non-zero value to avoid prolonged waits based on your requirements.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Login Request Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Login Request Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Proxy configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ProxyConfiguration">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object TTL</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long an object in the connection pool lives if the object is not used.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>30</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Object TTL</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Entries</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the amount of objects that live in the connection pool at a specific moment. When this number is reached and a new object is needed, no new object is created and the application waits until a connection is released.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>10</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username used to initialize the session.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to authenticate the user.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Security Token</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Userâs security token. It can be omitted if your IP is allowlisted on Salesforce.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authorization URL</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Web service URL responsible for user authentication. This is the URL for the endpoint configured to handle SOAP authentication requests. It defaults to the URL that contains the latest API version supported by the connector, for example, <code>https://login.salesforce.com/services/Soap/u/65.0</code>. Always use lowercase when specifying the protocol (e.g., http, https).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Session Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Value that identifies an active Salesforce session.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Service Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the service endpoint. This value is used only if the <strong>Session Id</strong> configuration property is used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>TLS configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### OAuth v2.0 Connection Type

Use OAuth 2.0 to delegate user authentication to the service hosting the user account. For more
information, refer to
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2) or
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors on CloudHub](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2-cloudhub).

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when establishing a connection to the remote service. Values less than one millisecond are converted to <code>0</code>. A value of <code>0</code> means wait indefinitely.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>0</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> parameter.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Login Request Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the consumer waits to receive the login request before the request times out. Values less than one millisecond are converted to <code>0</code>. A default value of <code>0</code> means an indefinite wait, so set a non-zero value to avoid prolonged waits based on your requirements.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Login Request Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Login Request Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Proxy configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ProxyConfiguration">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object TTL</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long an object in the connection pool lives if the object is not used.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>30</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Object TTL</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Entries</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the amount of objects that live in the connection pool at a specific moment. When this number is reached and a new object is needed, no new object is created and the application waits until a connection is released.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>10</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Api Version</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>API version used. Defaults to the latest API version supported by the connector.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>TLS configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>OAuth consumer key, as registered with the service provider.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Secret</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>OAuth consumer secret, as registered with the service provider.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authorization Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the service providerâs authorization endpoint. Always use lowercase when specifying the protocol (e.g., http, https).</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>https://login.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/authorize</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Access Token Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the service providerâs access token endpoint. Always use lowercase when specifying the protocol (e.g., http, https).</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>https://login.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Scopes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>OAuth scopes to request during the OAuth dance. This value defaults to the scopes in the annotation.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Resource Owner Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Resource owner ID to use with the authorization code grant type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Before</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the flow to execute immediately before starting the OAuth dance.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>After</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the flow to execute immediately after receiving an access token.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Listener Config</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configuration for the HTTP listener that listens for requests on the access token callback endpoint.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Callback Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path of the access token callback endpoint.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authorize Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path of the local HTTP endpoint that triggers the OAuth dance.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>External Callback Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL that the OAuth provider uses to access the callback endpoint if the endpoint is behind a proxy or accessed through an indirect URL.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the object store that stores data for each resource owner. If not configured, Mule uses the default object store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### OAuth JWT Connection Type

Uses OAuth 2.0 with a JSON Web token (JWT) request for user authentication.

> [!NOTE] Salesforce Connector supports providing a signed certificate only if you use the
> `SHA256withRSA` algorithm for the OAuth JWT connection type.

You can only enable **JSON Web Token-based access tokens** option on Salesforce server for REST API
calls. This option isnât compatible with Salesforce Connector.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when establishing a connection to the remote service. Values less than one millisecond are converted to <code>0</code>. A value of <code>0</code> means wait indefinitely.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>0</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> parameter.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Login Request Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the consumer waits to receive the login request before the request times out. Values less than one millisecond are converted to <code>0</code>. A default value of <code>0</code> means an indefinite wait, so set a non-zero value to avoid prolonged waits based on your requirements.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Login Request Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Login Request Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Proxy configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ProxyConfiguration">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object TTL</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long an object in the connection pool lives if the object is not used.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>30</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Object TTL</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Entries</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the amount of objects that live in the connection pool at a specific moment. When this number is reached and a new object is needed, no new object is created and the application waits until a connection is released.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>10</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Api Version</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>API version used. Defaults to the latest API version supported by the connector.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Consumer key for the Salesforce-connected app.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the keystore used to sign data during authentication.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Store Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password of the keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Certificate Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the certificate.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Principal</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username of the desired Salesforce user to take action on behalf of.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Token Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL pointing to the server that provides the authentication token.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>https://login.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Audience Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL that identifies the authorization server as an intended audience. The authorization server must verify that it is an intended audience for the token.</p></div><div><p>Use the authorization serverâs URL for the audience value if implementing for a community: <code>https://login.salesforce.com</code>, <code>https://test.salesforce.com</code>, or <code>https://community.force.com/customers</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>TLS configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### OAuth Client Credentials

Uses a client ID and client secret for authentication.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when establishing a connection to the remote service. Values less than one millisecond are converted to <code>0</code>. A value of <code>0</code> means wait indefinitely.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>0</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> parameter.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Login Request Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the consumer waits to receive the login request before the request times out. Values less than one millisecond are converted to <code>0</code>. A default value of <code>0</code> means an indefinite wait, so set a non-zero value to avoid prolonged waits based on your requirements.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Login Request Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Login Request Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Proxy configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ProxyConfiguration">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object TTL</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long an object in the connection pool lives if the object is not used.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>30</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Object TTL</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Entries</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the amount of objects that live in the connection pool at a specific moment. When this number is reached and a new object is needed, no new object is created and the application waits until a connection is released.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>10</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Api Version</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>API version used. Defaults to the latest API version supported by the connector.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>TLS configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Client ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The OAuth client ID as registered with the service provider.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Client Secret</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The OAuth client secret that corresponds to the client ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Token URL</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Service providerâs token endpoint URL. Always use lowercase when specifying the protocol (e.g., http, https).</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>https://{domain}.my.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Scopes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The OAuth scopes to request during the OAuth dance.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the object store that stores data for each resource owner. If not configured, Mule uses the default object store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### OAuth Username Password Connection Type

Uses OAuth 2.0 with a user and password for authentication.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when establishing a connection to the remote service. Values less than one millisecond are converted to <code>0</code>. A value of <code>0</code> means wait indefinitely.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>0</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> parameter.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Login Request Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the consumer waits to receive the login request before the request times out. Values less than one millisecond are converted to <code>0</code>. A default value of <code>0</code> means an indefinite wait, so set a non-zero value to avoid prolonged waits based on your requirements.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Login Request Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Login Request Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Proxy configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ProxyConfiguration">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object TTL</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long an object in the connection pool lives if the object is not used.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>30</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Object TTL</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Entries</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the amount of objects that live in the connection pool at a specific moment. When this number is reached and a new object is needed, no new object is created and the application waits until a connection is released.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>10</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Api Version</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>API version used. Defaults to the latest API version supported by the connector.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Consumer key for the Salesforce-connected app.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Secret</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Your applicationâs client secret (consumer secret in Remote Access Detail).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username used to initialize the session.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to authenticate the user.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Security Token</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Userâs security token. It can be omitted if your IP is allowlisted on Salesforce.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Token Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL pointing to the server that provides the authentication token.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>https://login.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>TLS configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### OAuth SAML Connection Type

> [!NOTE] OAuth SAML currently uses only Salesforce as an identity provider. Refer to
> [Salesforce as a SAML Identity Provider](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=xcloud.sso_sfdc_idp_saml_parent.htm&type=5)
> to enable Salesforce as an identity provider.

Uses OAuth 2.0 with a signed SAML 2.0 assertion to request an access token.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when establishing a connection to the remote service. Values less than one millisecond are converted to <code>0</code>. A value of <code>0</code> means wait indefinitely.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>0</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> parameter.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Login Request Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the consumer waits to receive the login request before the request times out. Values less than one millisecond are converted to <code>0</code>. A default value of <code>0</code> means an indefinite wait, so set a non-zero value to avoid prolonged waits based on your requirements.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Login Request Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Login Request Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Proxy configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ProxyConfiguration">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object TTL</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long an object in the connection pool lives if the object is not used.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>30</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Object TTL</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Entries</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the amount of objects that live in the connection pool at a specific moment. When this number is reached and a new object is needed, no new object is created and the application waits until a connection is released.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>10</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Api Version</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>API version used. Defaults to the latest API version supported by the connector.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Consumer key for the Salesforce-connected app.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the keystore used to sign data during authentication.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Store Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password of the keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Certificate Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the certificate.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Principal</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username of the desired Salesforce user to take action on behalf of.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Token Endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL pointing to the server that provides the authentication token.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>https://login.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>TLS configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Sources

- [On Deleted Object](#deleted-object-listener)
- [On Modified Object](#modified-object-listener)
- [On New Object](#new-object-listener)
- [Replay Channel Listener](#replay-channel-listener)
- [Replay Topic Listener](#replay-topic-listener)

> [!NOTE] The **On Deleted Object**, **On Modified Object**, and **On New Object** sources in
> Salesforce Connector use Object Store to save watermarks. You might experience limitations that
> are specific to the Object Store implementation you are using (Object Store for CloudHub
> deployments or Object Store for on-premises deployments), so configure Object Store to suit your
> needs.  
> For more information, see
> [The Different Types of Object Stores Explained](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=001123185&type=1).

### On Deleted Object

`<salesforce:deleted-object-listener>`

Source that adds one or more `whereConditions` in the query calling the method.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Triggers Query</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Query used to filter the trigger results, for example, <code>Name='Filter'</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Since</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specify a date in the <code>YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss</code> format, for example, <code>2017-03-17 16:30:40</code>. If this field is empty, this source retrieves the selected objects from the time the Mule app started.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Primary Node Only</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Determines whether to execute this source on only the primary node when running Mule instances in a cluster. This parameter doesnât have any impact on non-cluster mode with multiple workers.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Scheduling Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#fixed-frequency">Fixed Frequency</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#cron">Cron</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the scheduler that triggers the polling.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Redelivery Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the consumer waits for a response before it times out.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### On Modified Object

`<salesforce:modified-object-listener>`

Source for modified objects.

### On New Object

`<salesforce:new-object-listener>`

Source for new objects.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Object type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Triggers Query</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Query used to filter the trigger results, for example, <code>Name='Filter'</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Since</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specify a date in the <code>YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss</code> format, for example, <code>2017-03-17 16:30:40</code>. If this field is empty, this source retrieves the selected objects from the time the Mule app started.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Primary Node Only</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Determines whether to execute this source on only the primary node when running Mule instances in a cluster. This parameter doesnât have any impact on non-cluster mode with multiple workers.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Scheduling Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#fixed-frequency">Fixed Frequency</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#cron">Cron</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the scheduler that triggers the polling.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Redelivery Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the consumer waits for a response before it times out.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#sfdc-config)

### Replay Channel Listener

`<salesforce:replay-channel-listener>`

Source that subscribes to a streaming channel. First, the streaming channel must be published, and
after that, a subscription to that streaming channel must be created. Salesforce allows you to
subscribe to a nonexistent streaming channel but wonât send you notifications after the topic is
created.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Channel</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the streaming channel to subscribe to.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cache Events In Memory</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the processing flow is slow, cache events in memory to reduce quota consumption.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Replay Option</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ONLY_NEW</p></li><li><p>ALL</p></li><li><p>FROM_REPLAY_ID</p></li><li><p>FROM_LAST_REPLAY_ID</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>ONLY_NEW: -1</p><div><p>Subscriber receives new events that are broadcast after the client subscribes.</p></div></li><li><p>ALL: -2</p><div><p>Subscriber receives all events, including past events that are within the 24-hour retention window and new events sent after subscription.</p></div></li><li><p>FROM_REPLAY_ID</p><div><p>Subscriber receives only events that have a replay ID value greater than the specified replay ID.</p></div></li><li><p>FROM_LAST_REPLAY_ID</p><div><p>Subscriber uses the highest replay ID stored in the object store regardless of whether it is processed successfully or not.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Replay Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Subscriber receives all events after the event specified by this replay ID value. The value is ignored if the replay option is set to <code>ALL</code> or <code>ONLY_NEW</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Replay Failed Events If Any or Resume from Last Replay Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, the source starts consuming data with any failed events (if any) or starts consuming data from the last replay ID that is processed successfully.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>The size (in bytes) of the event queue (DEPRECATED)</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>In previous versions, this field was used to specify the size of the event queue which can lead to memory issues. Now, the messages are streamed and no buffering is needed.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Primary Node Only</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Determines whether to execute this source on only the primary node when running Mule instances in a cluster. This parameter doesnât have any impact on non-cluster mode with multiple workers.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>true</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Throw Exception When Organization Daily Limit Exceeded</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether the connector throws an exception when the application is deployed if the organization daily limit for events processing is exceeded.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#non-repeatable-stream">Non-Repeatable Stream</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Redelivery Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Attributes.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[message.attributes]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#sfdc-config)

### Replay Topic Listener

`<salesforce:replay-topic-listener>`

Salesforce stores events for 24 hours and enables you to retrieve stored and new events. Source in
which subscribers choose which events to receive by using replay options.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Topic</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the topic to subscribe to.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cache Events In Memory</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the processing flow is slow, cache events in memory to reduce quota consumption.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Replay Option</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ONLY_NEW</p></li><li><p>ALL</p></li><li><p>FROM_REPLAY_ID</p></li><li><p>FROM_LAST_REPLAY_ID</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>ONLY_NEW: -1</p><div><p>Subscriber receives new events that are broadcast after the client subscribes.</p></div></li><li><p>ALL: -2</p><div><p>Subscriber receives all events, including past events that are within the 24-hour retention window and new events sent after subscription.</p></div></li><li><p>FROM_REPLAY_ID</p><div><p>Subscriber receives only events that have a replay ID value greater than the specified replay ID.</p></div></li><li><p>FROM_LAST_REPLAY_ID</p><div><p>Subscriber uses the highest replay ID stored in the object store regardless of whether it is processed successfully or not.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Replay Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Subscriber receives all events after the event specified by this replay ID value. The value is ignored if the replay option is set to <code>ALL</code> or <code>ONLY_NEW</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Replay Failed Events If Any or Resume from Last Replay Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, the source starts consuming data with any failed events (if any) or starts consuming data from the last replay ID that is processed successfully.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Primary Node Only</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Determines whether to execute this source on only the primary node when running Mule instances in a cluster. This parameter doesnât have any impact on non-cluster mode with multiple workers.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>true</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Throw Exception When Organization Daily Limit Exceeded</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether the connector throws an exception when the application is deployed if the organization daily limit for events processing is exceeded.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#non-repeatable-stream">Non-Repeatable Stream</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Redelivery Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Attributes.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[message.attributes]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#sfdc-config)

## Operations

> [!NOTE] If you set `LocalDate` for a field in the payload, the input time is automatically
> converted to `GMT` or the serverâs time zone based on the Gregorian calendar. This conversion
> impacts date calculations or comparisons in Salesforce Connector operations, as `LocalDate` is
> adjusted to align with the serverâs time zone or defaults to GMT.

### Abort Job

`<salesforce:abort-job>`

Aborts an open job given its ID.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Job ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Job ID that identifies the job to abort.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the consumer waits for a response before it times out.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#JobInfo">Job Info</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#sfdc-config)

#### Throws

- SALESFORCE:LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SALESFORCE:CONNECTIVITY
- SALESFORCE:INVALID_RESPONSE
- SALESFORCE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SALESFORCE:TIMEOUT
- SALESFORCE:MUTUAL_AUTHENTICATION_FAILED
- SALESFORCE:NOT_FOUND
- SALESFORCE:INVALID_INPUT

### Abort Job Bulk Api V2

`<salesforce:abort-job-bulk-api-v2>`

Aborts an ongoing Bulk API v2 Job. This call uses the Bulk API v2.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Job ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the job.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the consumer waits for a response before it times out.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Error Mappings</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set of error mappings.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#BulkJobState">Bulk Job State</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#sfdc-config)

#### Throws

- SALESFORCE:LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SALESFORCE:CONNECTIVITY
- SALESFORCE:INVALID_RESPONSE
- SALESFORCE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SALESFORCE:TIMEOUT
- SALESFORCE:MUTUAL_AUTHENTICATION_FAILED
- SALESFORCE:NOT_FOUND
- SALESFORCE:INVALID_INPUT

### Abort Query Job Bulk Api V2

`<salesforce:abort-query-job-bulk-api-v2>`

Abort the indicated query job. This call uses the Bulk API v2.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the query job to abort.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the consumer waits for a response before it times out.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Error Mappings</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set of error mappings.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#QueryJobInfo">Query Job Info</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#sfdc-config)

#### Throws

- SALESFORCE:LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SALESFORCE:CONNECTIVITY
- SALESFORCE:INVALID_RESPONSE
- SALESFORCE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SALESFORCE:TIMEOUT
- SALESFORCE:MUTUAL_AUTHENTICATION_FAILED
- SALESFORCE:NOT_FOUND
- SALESFORCE:INVALID_INPUT

### Batch Info

`<salesforce:batch-info>`

Accesses the latest BatchInfo of a submitted BatchInfo. Enables you to track the execution status.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Batch info</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#BatchInfo">Batch Info</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>BatchInfo that is monitored.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>XML</p></li><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>ZIP_XML</p></li><li><p>ZIP_JSON</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Content type used at job creation.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>XML</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the consumer waits for a response before it times out.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Error Mappings</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set of error mappings.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#BatchInfo">Batch Info</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#sfdc-config)

#### Throws

- SALESFORCE:LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- SALESFORCE:CONNECTIVITY
- SALESFORCE:INVALID_RESPONSE
- SALESFORCE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SALESFORCE:TIMEOUT
- SALESFORCE:MUTUAL_AUTHENTICATION_FAILED
- SALESFORCE:NOT_FOUND
- SALESFORCE:INVALID_INPUT

### Batch Info List

`<salesforce:batch-info-list>`

Gets information about all batches in a job.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Job Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the job to retrieve batch information for.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>XML</p></li><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>ZIP_XML</p></li><li><p>ZIP_JSON</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Content type used at job creation.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>XML</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the consumer waits for a response before it times out.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Error Mappings</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set of error mappings.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Batch Result

`<salesforce:batch-result>`

Accesses `com.sforce.async.BatchResult` of a submitted BatchInfo.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to Info</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>com.sforce.async.BatchInfo</code> that is monitored.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>XML</p></li><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>ZIP_XML</p></li><li><p>ZIP_JSON</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Content type used at job
