Match record.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Additional Information</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#AdditionalInformationMap">Additional Information Map</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Additional information.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Field Diffs</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#FieldDiff">Field Diff</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Field differences.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Match Confidence</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Match confidence.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Record</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Record.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Match Result

Match result.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Entity Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Entity type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Errors</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#SimpleError">Simple Error</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Match Engine</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Match engine name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Match Records</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#MatchRecord">Match Record</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Match records.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Rule</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Rule name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Size.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Success</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Determines if successful or not.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Merge Record

Merge record.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Master Record</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Master record that contains at least the ID and the type of the object that other records merge into. Optionally, provide the fields and their values to update.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Record To Merge Ids</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Additional records to merge into the primary record. Minimum of one record and maximum of two records.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Additional Information Map</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Field-value map. When a portal user ID is merged, it must contain two entries: <code>name: PortalUserId</code> and <code>value: ID of the portal user</code>. In all other merge cases, set to null or leave empty.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Merge Result

Merge result.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Errors</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#Error">Error</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Merged Record Ids</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Merged record IDs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Success</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Determines if successful or not.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Updated Related Ids</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Updated related IDs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Non-Repeatable Stream

Configures the non-repeatable streaming strategy, in which Mule disables repeatable streams. Use
this strategy if your use case requires reading the content only once.

In some cases, you might want to disable the repeatable stream functionality and use non-repeatable
streams, which can have less performance overhead, memory use, and cost.

### Pick List Entry

Pick list entry.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Active</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates active or not.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Default Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates default value.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Label</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Label.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Valid For</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates when valid for.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Value.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

Configures a standard reconnection strategy, which specifies how often to reconnect and how many
reconnection attempts the connector source or operation can make.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to attempt to reconnect, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>false</code>, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How many reconnection attempts the Mule app can make.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

Configures a forever reconnection strategy by which the connector source or operation attempts to
reconnect at a specified frequency for as long as the Mule app runs.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to attempt to reconnect, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>false</code>, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

Configures a reconnection strategy for an operation.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>What to do if, when an app is deployed, a connectivity test does not pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Allow the deployment to fail.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Ignore the results of the connectivity test.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Reconnection strategy to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Record Type Info

Record type information.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Available</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates whether available or not.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Default Record Type Mapping</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates default record type mapping.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Master</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates master.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy

Configures the redelivery policy for executing requests that generate errors. You can add a
redelivery policy to any source in a flow.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Redelivery Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum number of times that a redelivered request can be processed unsuccessfully before returning a REDELIVERY_EXHAUSTED error.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Digest Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Secure hashing algorithm to use if the <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> field is <code>true</code>. If the payload of the message is a Java object, Mule ignores this value and returns the value that the payloadâs <code>hashCode()</code> returned.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Identifier</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicyMessageIdentifier">Redelivery Policy Message Identifier</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines which strategy is used to identify the messages.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ObjectStore</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the object store that stores the redelivery counter for each message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy Message Identifier

Configures the redelivery policy message identifier type.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Use Secure Hash</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use a secure hash algorithm to identify a redelivered message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id Expression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines one or more expressions to use to determine when a message has been redelivered. This property may only be set if the <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> field is false.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Iterable

Repeatable file store iterable.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>In Memory Objects</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum amount of instances to keep in memory. If more than that is required, content on the disk is buffered.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit for the <strong>In Memory Size</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Stream

Configures the repeatable file-store streaming strategy by which Mule keeps a portion of the stream
content in memory. If the stream content is larger than the configured buffer size, Mule backs up
the bufferâs content to disk and then clears the memory.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>In Memory Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum amount of memory that the stream can use for data. If the amount of memory exceeds this value, Mule buffers the content to disk. To optimize performance:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Configure a larger buffer size to avoid the number of times Mule needs to write the buffer on disk. This increases performance, but it also limits the number of concurrent requests your application can process, because it requires additional memory.</p></li><li><p>Configure a smaller buffer size to decrease memory load at the expense of response time.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit for the <strong>In Memory Size</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Iterable

Repeatable in memory iterable.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amount of instances to initially keep in memory to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than can fit into this buffer, then the buffer expands according to the <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong> attribute, with an upper limit of <strong>Max In Memory Size</strong>. Default value is 100 instances.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This is by how much the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of <code>0</code> or lower means that the buffer must not expand, meaning that a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error is raised when the buffer gets full. Default value is 100 instances.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum amount of memory to use. If more than that is used, a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error is raised. A value lower than or equal to <code>0</code> means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Stream

Configures the in-memory streaming strategy by which the request fails if the data exceeds the MAX
buffer size. Always run performance tests to find the optimal buffer size for your specific use
case.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Initial amount of memory to allocate to the data stream. If the streamed data exceeds this value, the buffer expands by <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong>, with an upper limit of <strong>Max In Memory Size value</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amount by which the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of <code>0</code> or lower specifies that the buffer canât expand.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum amount of memory to use. If more than that is used, a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error is raised. A value lower than or equal to <code>0</code> means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit for the <strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong>, <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong>, and <strong>Buffer Unit</strong> fields.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reset Password Result

Reset password result.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password to authenticate against the proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Result

Result.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Errors</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#Error">Error</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Array of the Error type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Success</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Determines if successful or not.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fields</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Fields.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Status Code</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Status code.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

Configures standard revocation checks for TLS certificates.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Only End Entities</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Which elements to verify in the certificate chain:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Verify only the last element in the certificate chain.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Verify all elements in the certificate chain.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prefer Crls</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How to check certificate validity:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Check the Certification Revocation List (CRL) for certificate validity.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Use the Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP) to check certificate validity.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>No Fallback</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use the secondary method to check certificate validity:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Use the method that wasnât specified in the <strong>Prefer Crls</strong> field (the secondary method) to check certificate validity.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Do not use the secondary method to check certificate validity.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Soft Fail</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>What to do if the revocation server canât be reached or is busy:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Avoid verification failure.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Allow the verification to fail.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### TLS

Configures TLS to provide secure communications for the Mule app.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Protocols</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Cipher Suites</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trust Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Truststore</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Keystore</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Revocation Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#standard-revocation-check">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#custom-ocsp-responder">Custom OCSP Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#crl-file">CRL File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a revocation checking mechanism.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Truststore

Configures the truststore for TLS.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the truststore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm that the truststore uses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Insecure</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, Mule stops performing certificate validations. Setting this to <code>true</code> can make connections vulnerable to attacks.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Upsert Metadata Result

Upsert metadata result.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Created</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates whether created or not.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Errors</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#Error">Error</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Full Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Full name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Success</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Determines if successful or not.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Using Anypoint Studio to Configure Salesforce Connector 11.4

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Studio (Studio) editors help you design and update your Mule applications, properties, and
configuration files.

To add and configure a connector in Studio:

1.  [Create a Mule project](#create-mule-project).
2.  [Add the connector to your Mule project](#add-connector-to-project).
3.  [Configure a source for the connectorâs flow](#configure-input-source).
4.  [Add a connector operation to the flow](#add-connector-operation).
5.  [Configure a global element for the connector](#configure-global-element)
6.  [Configure the other connector fields](#configure-other-fields).

When you run the connector, you can view the app log to check for problems, as described in
[View the App Log](#view-app-log).

If you are new to configuring connectors in Studio, see
[Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio).
If, after reading this topic, you need additional information about the connector fields, see the
[Salesforce Reference](salesforce-connector-reference).

## Create a Mule Project

In Studio, create a new Mule project in which to add and configure the connector:

1.  In Studio, select **File > New > Mule Project**.
2.  Enter a name for your Mule project and click **Finish**.

## Add the Connector to Your Mule Project

Add Salesforce Connector to your Mule project to automatically populate the XML code with the
connectorâs namespace and schema location and add the required dependencies to the projectâs
`pom.xml` file:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
2.  In **Add Modules to Project**, type `salesforce` in the search field.
3.  Click the connector name in **Available modules**.
4.  Click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.

Adding a connector to a Mule project in Studio does not make that connector available to other
projects in your Studio workspace.

## Configure a Source

A source initiates a flow when a specified condition is met. A channel or topic must be published to
Salesforce before a subscription to the channel can be created.

You can configure one of these sources to use with Salesforce Connector:

- **On Deleted Object**

  Initiates your app when a Salesforce user deletes an object. Possible object types are `Contact`,
  `Lead`, `Account`, `Contracts`, `Opportunity`, or the name of a custom object.

- **On Modified Object**

  Initiates your app when a Salesforce user modifies an object. Possible object types are `Contact`,
  `Lead`, `Account`, `Contracts`, `Opportunity`, or the name of a custom object.

- **On New Object**

  Initiates your app when a Salesforce user creates a new object. Possible object types are
  `Contact`, `Lead`, `Account`, `Contracts`, `Opportunity`, or the name of a custom object.

- **Replay channel listener**

  Subscribes to a streaming channel. This source provides channel-related notification settings for
  new events and events stored by Salesforce within the 24-hour retention period. Events that occur
  in the specified channel source trigger the flow.

- **Replay topic listener**

  Subscribes to a topic. This source provides topic-related notification settings for new events and
  events stored by Salesforce within the 24-hour retention period. Events that occur on records in
  the specified topic trigger the flow.

- **HTTP > Listener**.

  Initiates a flow each time it receives a request on the configured host and port

- **Scheduler**

  Initiates a flow when a time-based condition is met.

> [!NOTE] A channel or topic must be published to Salesforce before a subscription to the channel
> can be created.

### Example of Configuring a Source

For example, to configure **On New Object** as a source:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **On New Object**.
2.  Drag **On New Object** to the Studio canvas.
3.  In the On New Object properties window, specify a value for the **Path** field.
4.  Optionally change the value of the **Display Name** field.
5.  Select an object type and optionally specify a UTC value in the **Since** field.

    If the server is in a different time zone, you must specify a value in the **Since** field. If
    you leave the field empty, the connector uses the local timestamp.

6.  Click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Connector configuration** field to configure a global
    element that can be used by all instances of the **On New Object** source in the app.
7.  In the **TLS** tab, optionally specify the TLS information for the connector.
8.  In the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
9.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
10. Click **OK**.

### Redelivery Policy

To configure a redelivery policy for a source, set the **Redelivery** field in the sourceâs
properties window to the maximum number of redelivery attempts allowed after an initial failure.

You can specify an object store for this policy. If you donât specify an object store, Mule creates
a nonpersistent object store. The number of transactions used to interact with the object store
varies based on the number of retries configured for the redelivery policy.

For more information about configuring a redelivery policy, see
[Redelivery Policy](../../mule-runtime/latest/redelivery-policy).

## Add a Connector Operation to the Flow

When you add a connector operation to your flow, you immediately define a specific operation for
that connector to perform.

To add an operation for Salesforce Connector, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **Salesforce** and then select the desired operation.
2.  Drag the operation onto the Studio canvas to the right of the source.

## Configure a Global Element for the Connector

When you configure a connector, configure a global element that all instances of that connector in
the app can use. Configuring a global element requires you to provide the authentication credentials
the connector uses to access the target Salesforce system.

You can reference a configuration file that contains ANT-style property placeholders (recommended),
or you can enter your authorization credentials in the global configuration properties. For
information about the benefits of using property placeholders and how to configure them, see
[Anypoint Connector Configuration](../../connectors/introduction/intro-connector-configuration-overview).

To configure a global element for Salesforce Connector, follow these steps:

1.  Select the name of the connector in the Studio canvas.
2.  In the configuration screen for the operation, click the plus sign (+) next to the **Connector
    configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
3.  In the **General** tab, select one of these authentication methods for your connection:
    - [Basic authentication](#basic)
    - [OAuth 2.0 authentication](#oauth)
    - [OAuth JWT authentication](#oauth-jwt)
    - [OAuth Client Credentials authentication](#oauth-client-credentials)
    - [OAuth SAML authentication](#oauth-saml)
    - [OAuth Username Password authentication](#oauth-username-password)

4.  In the **Apex** tab, optionally configure [Apex Settings](#apexsets).
5.  In the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
6.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
7.  Click **OK**.

### Authentication

For all OAuth connections, you can specify an object store that stores each resource ownerâs ID
data. If you donât specify an object store, Mule automatically provisions the default object store.
The app interacts with the object store automatically when a new resource owner is authenticated,
the access token is refreshed, or the access token is invalidated.

All authentication types support mutual TLS. To use mutual TLS, you need a keystore file and a
password for the file. See
[Generate a Keystore File](salesforce-connector-config-topics#generate-keystore) for more
information.

In Studio, when you configure the connectorâs authentication method, specify the path to the
keystore file and the TLS password on the **Security** tab.

To set up a mutual TLS certificate in your Salesforce environment, see
[Set Up a Mutual Authentication Certificate](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?language=en_US&id=security_keys_uploading_mutual_auth_cert.htm&type=5).

#### Basic Authentication

Basic authentication uses a username and password for authentication.

Important fields in the **General** tab include the following:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Salesforce username.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Corresponding password.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Security token</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Corresponding security token.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Callback path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Path for the callback, for example, <code>/callback</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authorization URL</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Web service URL responsible for user authentication. This is the URL for the endpoint configured to handle SOAP authentication requests. It defaults to the URL that contains the latest API version supported by the connector, for example, <code>https://login.salesforce.com/services/Soap/u/60.0</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### OAuth 2.0 Authentication

Use OAuth 2.0 to delegate user authentication to the service hosting the user account. For more
information, refer to
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2) or
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors on CloudHub](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2-cloudhub).

OAuth 2.0 delegates user authentication to the service hosting the user account.

Important fields in the **General** tab include the following:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Consumer key for the Salesforce-connected app. For information about generating a consumer key, refer to the <a href="https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=xcloud.connected_app_rotate_consumer_details.htm&amp;type=5">Salesforce documentation</a>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer secret</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Password for the keystore.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Listener config</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Configuration for the listener, for example, `HTTP_Listener_config.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Callback path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Path for the callback, for example, <code>/callback</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authorize path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Path for authorization, for example, <code>/authorize</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>External call back url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Callback URL, for example, <code>http://localhost:8085/callback</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

Important fields in the **Advanced** tab include the following:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Api version</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>API version used. Defaults to the latest API version supported by the connector.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### OAuth JWT Authentication

OAuth JWT uses OAuth 2.0 with a JSON Web Token (JWT) request for user authentication.

Important fields in the **General tab** include the following:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Consumer key for the Salesforce-connected app. For information about generating a consumer key, refer to the <a href="https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=xcloud.connected_app_rotate_consumer_details.htm&amp;type=5">Salesforce documentation</a>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the keystore that signs data during authentication. See <a href="salesforce-connector-config-topics#generate-keystore">Generate a Keystore File</a>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Store password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Password for the keystore.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Corresponding password.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Certificate Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the certificate to use if the specified keystore contains multiple certificates.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Principal</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Username of the Salesforce user.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

Important fields in the **Advanced** tab include the following:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Api version</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>API version used. Defaults to the latest API version supported by the connector.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### OAuth Client Credentials Authentication

OAuth Client Credentials uses a client ID and client secret for authentication.

Important fields in the **General tab** include the following:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Client id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>The OAuth client ID as registered with the service provider.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Client secret</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>The OAuth client secret that corresponds to the client ID.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

Important fields in the **Advanced** tab include the following:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Api version</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>API version used. Defaults to the latest API version supported by the connector.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### OAuth SAML Authentication

> [!NOTE] OAuth SAML currently uses only Salesforce as an identity provider. Refer to
> [Salesforce as a SAML Identity Provider](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=xcloud.sso_sfdc_idp_saml_parent.htm&type=5)
> to enable Salesforce as an identity provider.

OAuth SAML uses OAuth 2.0 with a signed SAML 2.0 assertion to request an access token.

Important fields in the **General** tab include the following:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Consumer key for the Salesforce-connected app. For information about generating a consumer key, refer to the <a href="https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=xcloud.connected_app_rotate_consumer_details.htm&amp;type=5">Salesforce documentation</a>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the keystore that signs data during authentication. See <a href="salesforce-connector-config-topics#generate-keystore">Generate a Keystore File</a>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Store password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Password for the keystore.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Corresponding password.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Certificate Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the certificate to use if the specified keystore contains multiple certificates.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Principal</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Username of the Salesforce user.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

Important fields in the **Advanced** tab include the following:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Api version</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>API version used. Defaults to the latest API version supported by the connector.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### OAuth Username Password Authentication

OAuth Username Password authentication uses OAuth 2.0 with a user and password for authentication.

Important fields in the **General** tab include the following:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Consumer key for the Salesforce-connected app. For information about generating a consumer key, refer to the <a href="https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=xcloud.connected_app_rotate_consumer_details.htm&amp;type=5">Salesforce documentation</a>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer secret</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Consumer secret for the connector to access Salesforce.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Salesforce username.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Corresponding password.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Security token</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Corresponding security token.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

Important fields in the **Advanced** tab include the following:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Api version</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>API version used. Defaults to the latest API version supported by the connector.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Apex Settings

Salesforce Connector gets the names of the Apex classes and the associated methods that can be
invoked when you connect to Salesforce.

To configure Apex settings in Studio:

1.  Click the **Apex** tab in the global element properties screen.
2.  Enter the following information:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fetch All Apex SOAP Metadata</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Fetches the metadata of all the Apex SOAP classes. Takes precedence over Apex Class Name settings.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Fetch All Apex REST Metadata</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Fetches the metadata of all the Apex REST classes. Takes precedence over Apex Class Name settings.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Apex Class Names</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>List of Apex class names to use for limiting the set of classes you fetch and the methods that they expose. This setting can accelerate the fetch process if you donât need to fetch many classes. Valid values:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><strong>None</strong></p><div><p>No classes are specified for DataSense to acquire.</p></div></li><li><p><strong>From a message</strong></p><div><p>Enables you to specify the class name using an expression.</p></div></li><li><p><strong>Create object manually</strong></p><div><p>Enables you to create a list of class names for DataSense to acquire. DataSense acquires only those classes and their associated methods.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Configure Additional Connector Fields

After you configure a global element for Salesforce Connector, configure the other required fields
for the connector.

The following example shows four operations of the many you can configure for Salesforce Connector,
as well as the important fields for these operations:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Operation</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Create</p></td><td><div><div><p>Adds one or more new records to your organizationâs data. Important fields include:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><strong>Type</strong></p><div><p>Salesforce object type</p></div></li><li><p><strong>Records</strong></p><div><p>Function editor expression</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query</p></td><td><div><div><p>Executes a query against the specified object and returns data that matches the specified criteria. Important fields include:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><strong>Salesforce query</strong></p><div><p>Salesforce query to retrieve objects</p></div></li><li><p><strong>Parameters</strong></p><div><p>Values for placeholders in the Salesforce query</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Update</p></td><td><div><div><p>Updates one or more existing records in your organizationâs data. Important fields include:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><strong>Type</strong></p><div><p>Salesforce object type</p></div></li><li><p><strong>Records</strong></p><div><p>Function editor expression that produces a collection of Salesforce objects to update</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delete</p></td><td><div><div><p>Deletes one or more records from your organizationâs data. Important fields include:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><strong>Records To Delete IDs</strong></p><div><p>Function editor expression that produces a collection of Salesforce objects to delete</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## View the App Log

To check for problems, you can view the app log as follows:

- If youâre running the app from Anypoint Platform, the output is visible in the Anypoint Studio
  console window.
- If youâre running the app using Mule from the command line, the app log is visible in your OS
  console.

Unless the log file path is customized in the appâs log file (`log4j2.xml`), you can also view the
app log in the default location `MULE_HOME/logs/<app-name>.log`.

## Next Step

After completing Studio setup, see
[Additional Configuration Information](salesforce-connector-config-topics).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)

# Troubleshooting Salesforce Connector 11.4

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

To troubleshoot Anypoint Connector for Salesforce (Salesforce Connector), become familiar with the
information about performing general troubleshooting and interpreting commonly thrown exception
messages.

## Enable SSL Logging

Enable SSL logs to investigate an issue related to TLS communications.

Set your SSL log level depending on your required level of detail:

1.  Enable only SSL log details:

    `javax.net.debug=ssl`

2.  Enable only handshaking details. (The handshake protocol is a series of messages exchanged over
    the record protocol.)

    `javax.net.debug=ssl:handshake`

3.  Enable handshaking details and SSL details at the same time:

    `javax.net.debug=ssl, handshake`

4.  Enable the dumping of all details and traffic data:

    `javax.net.debug=all`

    > [!NOTE] This option is very verbose and under normal circumstances is not necessary.

> [!WARNING] SSL logging results in a performance impact for HTTPS or other TLS connections. Enable
> SSL logging only to troubleshoot a specific issue and do not enable it for extended periods. SSL
> logging produces a significant amount of log messages and can inundate your log file if it is left
> enabled and unattended.

To enable SSL logs:

Set the debug parameter as an argument in the runtime configuration for the application.

1.  In Studio, right-click on the project and select **Run > Run Configurations**.
2.  Go to the **Arguments** tab and add `-M-Djavax.net.debug=ssl` in the **VM arguments** section.

## Enable Verbose Exception Logging

Enable verbose exception logs to show a complete stack trace of the error instead of the default
truncated output:

1.  In Studio, right-click on the project and select **Run > Run Configurations**.
2.  Go to the **Arguments** tab and append the arguments in the **VM arguments** section to add the
    `mule.verbose.exceptions` property. For example:

    `-XX:PermSize=128M -XX:MaxPermSize=256M -Dmule.verbose.exceptions=true`

## Enable Verbose Logging

To get a better understanding of why an applicationâs interaction with Salesforce Connector is
failing, temporarily enable verbose logging for the connector.

Remember to always disable enhanced verbosity after troubleshooting, because it can affect your Mule
applicationâs performance.

To enable verbose logging in the configuration file:

1.  Access Anypoint Studio and navigate to the **Package Explorer** view.
2.  Open your applicationâs project name.
3.  Open the `src/main/resources` folder.
4.  Open the `log4j2.xml` file inside the folder.
5.  Add an `<AsyncLogger>` tag inside the `<Loggers>` tag:

    ```xml
    			<Loggers>
    				...
    				<AsyncLogger name="org.mule.extension.salesforce" level="DEBUG"/>
    				...
    			</Loggers>
    ```

6.  Save your application changes.
7.  Click the project name in **Package Explorer** and then click **Run** > **Run As** > **Mule
    Application**.

## Perform General Troubleshooting

Here is a list of general troubleshooting guidelines for Salesforce Connector:

- [Currency](#currency)
- [Evaluate Values in a Salesforce List](#evaluate-values-in-a-salesforce-list)
- [Insert Values in a Salesforce Connector List](#insert-values-in-a-salesforce-connector-list)
- [Japanese Support](#japanese-support)
- [Limits on API Calls](#limits-on-api-calls)
- [Opportunity Object](#opportunity-object)

### Currency

Currency values cannot exceed 18 characters in length.

When working with multiple currencies, you must know which currency your sObject uses to avoid
inaccurate entries. The default currency matches the location at the organization level.

### Evaluate Values in a Salesforce List

When using a value to evaluate against contents of an existing list field in Salesforce, use the
exact value in the list. For example, if you use the value `US` to evaluate against contents of a
list that contains the value `USA`, the evaluation works, but the result returns two values in the
list: one for `US` and one for `USA`.

### Insert Values in a Salesforce Connector List

Inserting dependent values into an existing list in Salesforce Connector does not always work. Test
to confirm this functionality.

### Japanese Support

Salesforce Connector supports Japanese characters for all operations, with the following exceptions:

- The **Publish Streaming Channel** operation does not support Japanese characters in the **Channel
  name** field, which can contain only alphanumeric characters, and the `_` or `/` characters. Refer
  to the Salesforce
  [StreamingChannel](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/atlas.en-us.228.0.object_reference.meta/object_reference/sforce_api_objects_streamingchannel.htm)
  documentation for more information.
- Metadata operations might return error messages if some fields contain Japanese characters. For
  example, creating custom objects or custom fields with a **Full Name** that contains Japanese
  characters triggers a `FIELD_INTEGRITY_EXCEPTION` error with the following message:  
  `The Object Name field can only contain underscores and alphanumeric characters. It must be unique, begin with a letter, not include spaces, not end with an underscore, and not contain two consecutive underscores.`

### Limits on API Calls

You must know the scope of the rate limiting policy that applies to your account so that you do not
exceed the number of allotted API calls per day.

### Opportunity Object

When extracting data from a Salesforce Opportunity object, be aware that a `quarter` in this context
is relative to the fiscal year of the organization and not necessarily to the calendar year.

## Understand Commonly Thrown Exceptions

Here is a list of commonly thrown exception messages and how to interpret them:

- SALESFORCE:COMMUNICATION

  A communication error occurs between systems.

- SALESFORCE:CONNECTIVITY

  A problem occurred and a connection could not be established. Review your Salesforce Connector
  configuration and reconnection strategy.

- SALESFORCE:INSUFFICIENT_PERMISSIONS

  The provided permissions are insufficient.

- SALESFORCE:INVALID_INPUT

  The provided input data for an operation is not valid.

- SALESFORCE:INVALID_RESPONSE

  The response from the server cannot be processed.

- SALESFORCE:LIMIT_EXCEEDED

  A specific limit is exceeded, for example, if you try to create more than 200 entities at once
  with the \*Create\* operation.

- SALESFORCE:MUTUAL_AUTHENTICATION_FAILED

  The mutual authentication failed.

- SALESFORCE:NOT_FOUND

  The server could not find the requested resource.

- SALESFORCE:OPERATION_NOT_SUPPORTED

  The operation is not supported.

- SALESFORCE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

  The maximum number of retries for the operation is reached.

- SALESFORCE:TIMEOUT

  A specific request takes longer than a configured amount of time to complete.

- SALESFORCE:UNAVAILABLE

  The server is unavailable.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Connector Reference](salesforce-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [Known Issues](https://help.salesforce.com/s/issues)
---

# Upgrading and Migrating Salesforce Connector to 11.x

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Upgrade Anypoint Connector for Salesforce (Salesforce Connector) from version 10.x to version 11.x.

## Supported Upgrade Paths

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>From Version</th><th>To Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>10.x</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>11.4</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Changes

### 11.3

- Salesforce Connector now supports Salesforce API v65.0.

### 11.2

- Salesforce Connector now supports Salesforce API v64.0.

### 11.1

- Salesforce Connector now supports Salesforce API v62.0 and v63.0.

### 11.0

This release contains the following changes:

- The **OAuth Client Credentials** connection type is added.
- The **Subscribe Channel Listener** source is removed. Use the **Replay Channel Listener** source
  instead by using the `ONLY_NEW` replay option.
- The **Subscribe Topic Listener** source is removed. Use the **Replay Topic Listener** source
  instead by using the `ONLY_NEW` replay option.
- The deprecated **Query Result Stream** operation is removed. Use both of these operations instead:
  - The **Query Result List** operation to return a list of result IDs for a specific batch.
  - The **Query Result Stream by Id** operation to return an InputStream with the query results for
    a specific result ID of a submitted BatchInfo.

## Upgrade Prerequisites

Before you perform the upgrade, you must:

1.  Create a backup of your files, data, and configuration in case you need to restore to the
    previous version.
2.  Install Salesforce Connector 11.x to replace the Salesforce operations that were previously
    included in Salesforce Connector 10.x.

## Upgrade Steps

Follow these steps to perform the upgrade from Salesforce Connector v10.x to Salesforce Connector
v11.x:

1.  In Anypoint Studio, create a Mule project.
2.  In the Mule Palette view, click **Search in Exchange**.
3.  In the **Add Dependencies to Project** window, enter `Salesforce` in the search field.
4.  In the **Available modules** section, select **Salesforce Connector - Mule 4** and click
    **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.
6.  Verify that the `salesforce-connector` dependency version is `11.x.x` in the `pom.xml` file,
    where `x.x` is the minor release and patch version number. For example, if you are upgrading to
    v11.0, the dependency version in the `pom.xml` file should be `11.0.0`.

Studio upgrades the connector automatically.

## Verify the Upgrade

After you install the latest version of the connector, follow these steps to verify the upgrade:

1.  In Studio, verify that there are no errors in the **Problems** or **Console** views.
2.  Check the project `pom.xml` file and verify that there are no problems.
3.  Test the connection and verify that the operations work.

## Troubleshooting

If there are problems with caching the parameters and caching the metadata, try restarting Studio.

## Revert the Upgrade

If it is necessary to revert to the previous version of Salesforce Connector, change the
`salesforce-connector` dependency version in the projectâs `pom.xml` file to the previous version.

You must update the projectâs `pom.xml` file in Anypoint Studio.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Salesforce Connector 11.4 XML and Maven Support

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

If you manually code a Mule app in XML either from the Anypoint Studio XML editor or a text editor,
perform the following actions to get access to the connector:

- Add the namespace for the connector to your XML flow.
- Add dependency information to your Maven `pom.xml` file.

In the Studio visual editor, when you drag a connector from the palette to the canvas, Studio
automatically populates the XML code with the connector namespace and schema location.

## Add a Namespace for the Connector

Paste the following code inside the `<mule>` tag of the header of your configuration XML:

```xml
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce/current/mule-salesforce.xsd
```

This example shows how the namespace statements are placed in the `mule` XML block:

```xml
<mule xmlns:salesforce="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce"
	xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce/current/mule-salesforce.xsd">
```

## Add a POM File Dependency

The Maven `pom.xml` file generated by Studio contains dependencies for a Mule app. When you code a
Mule app manually, include this XML snippet in your `pom.xml` file to enable access to this
connector.

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
  <artifactId>mule-salesforce-connector</artifactId>
  <version>x.x.x</version>
  <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>
```

Replace `x.x.x` with version information from Exchange. Ensure the connector version corresponds to
the one you are using.

To obtain the most up-to-date `pom.xml` file information:

1.  Go to [Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/).
2.  In Exchange, click **Login** and supply your Anypoint Platform username and password.
3.  In Exchange, search for `salesforce`.
4.  Select the connector.
5.  Click **Dependency Snippets** near the upper right of the screen.

## Next

After completing your namespace and `pom.xml` file, you can try the
[Salesforce Examples](salesforce-connector-examples).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
